You get those two things right and you will minimize your losses and you'll maximize your profits as you're scaling up your ads. To give you an example, if you are scaling up 20% a day, you're duplicating ad search, whichever strategy you choose to use, knowing when things go wrong and when to cut and when to minimize your losses makes such a difference in terms of being able to scale out confidently as well. Because you'll know if you're running a Facebook ads campaign, if things go wrong, you lose confidence quite quickly as well. And being able to spot the trends in your data and know when and how to react is so crucial as well. And as with any kind of marketplace, there are forces outside of your control which will have an impact on your day-to-day analysis and your day-to-day scaling of the account as well. So it may be that a big brand has put a lot of money into targeting your core audience and your costs rise and maybe your conversion rate goes down because a whole batch of competitors have come in or there's some other factors within Facebook that you haven't taken into account. So as much as we talk about campaign structure and funnels and optimizations and things like that, there is also a lot outside of your control. That's when your expectation doesn't match your experience and your ability to control your spend. And that's where many other problems happen.
